req\n}\n\n// Reference is a string which can refer to either a concrete\n// revision or it can be an indirect named reference.\ntype Reference string\n\n// ReferenceRequest is used to identify a namespace (\"default\")\n// is returned instead.\nfunc (n NamespaceRequest) String() string {\n\treturn n.Namespace()\n}\n\n// Namespace returns the resolved target namespace key string.\n// If the key,\n\t}\n\n\tcontainers.ApplyAll(&p.ReferenceRequest, opts...)\n\n\treturn p\n}\n\n// String returns a representation of the combined resource namespace and key separated by a '/'.\nfunc (p sub-resources with no key identifiers (e.g. rules and rollouts).\ntype IDRequest struct {\n\tReferenceRequest\n\tID string\n}\n\n// NewID constructs and configures a new *IDRequest with the provided generic container for the parameters required to perform a list operation.\n// It contains a generic type T intended for a list predicate.\n// It also contains a QueryParams object containing pagination constraints.\ntype ListRequest[P any] struct {\n\tPredicate   P\n\tQueryParams QueryParams\n}\n\n// ListOption is a function which can configure a ListRequest.\ntype ListOption[T any] func(*ListRequest[T])\n\n// ListWithQueryParamOptions takes a set of functional options for QueryParam and returns a ListOption\n// which applies them in order on the provided ListRequest.\nfunc ListWithQueryParamOptions[T any](opts ...QueryOption) ListOption[T] {\n\treturn func(r *ListRequest[T]) {\n\t\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\t\topt(&r.QueryParams)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// PageParameter is any type which exposes limit and page token\n// accessors used to identify pages.\ntype PageParameter interface {\n\tGetLimit() int32\n\tGetPageToken() string\n}\n\n// OffsetPageParameter is a type which exposes an additional offset\n// accessor for legacy paging implementations (page token supersedes).\ntype OffsetPageParameter interface {\n\tPageParameter\n\tGetOffset() int32\n}\n\n// ListWithParameters constructs a new ListRequest using the page parameters\n// exposed by the provided PageParameter implementation.\nfunc ListWithParameters[T any](t T, p PageParameter) *ListRequest[T] {\n\topts := []QueryOption{\n\t\tWithLimit(uint64(p.GetLimit())),\n\t\tWithPageToken(p.GetPageToken()),\n\t}\n\n\tif po, ok := p.(OffsetPageParameter); ok {\n\t\topts = append(opts, WithOffset(uint64(po.GetOffset())))\n\t}\n\n\treturn ListWithOptions[T](t, ListWithQueryParamOptions[T](opts...))\n}\n\n// ListWithOptions constructs a new ListRequest using the provided ListOption.\nfunc ListWithOptions[T any](t T, opts ...ListOption[T]) *ListRequest[T] {\n\treq := &ListRequest[T]{\n\t\tPredicate: t,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\topt(req)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// Reference is a string which can refer to either a concrete\n// revision or it can be an indirect named reference.\ntype Reference string\n\n// ReferenceRequest is used to identify a WithReference sets the provided reference identifier on a *ReferenceRequest.\nfunc WithReference(ref string) containers.Option[ReferenceRequest] {\n\treturn func(rp *ReferenceRequest) {\n\t\tif ref != \"\" {\n\t\t\trp.Reference = Reference(ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// ListWithReference is a ListOption constrained to ReferenceRequest types.\n// It sets the reference on the resulting list request.\nfunc ListWithReference(ref string) ListOption[ReferenceRequest] {\n\treturn func(rp *ListRequest[ReferenceRequest]) {\n\t\tif ref != \"\" {\n\t\t\trp.Predicate.Reference = Reference(ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// NamespaceRequest is used to identify a request predicated by both a revision and a namespace.\n// This is used to identify namespaces and list resources directly key}\n\n\tcontainers.ApplyAll(&p.ReferenceRequest, opts...)\n\n\treturn p\n}\n\n// String returns the resolved target namespace key string.\n// If the underlying key is the empty string, the default namespace (\"default\")\n// is returned instead.\nfunc (n NamespaceRequest) String() string {\n\treturn n.Namespace()\n}\n\n// Namespace returns the resolved target namespace key string.\n// If the \\treturn resp, nil\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\\n224: // on the match {\\n226: \\tconstraintMatches := 0\\n227: \\n228: \\tvar reason string\\n229: \\n230: \\tfor _, c := range constraints {\\n231: \\t\\tv := evalCtx[c.Property]\\n232: \\n233: \\t\\tvar (\\n234: \\t\\t\\tmatch bool\\n235: \\t\\t\\terr   error\\n236: \\t\\t)\\n237: \\n238: \\t\\tswitch c.Type {\\n239: \\t\\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:\\n240: \\t\\t\\tmatch = c.Property), zap.Error(err))\\n255: \\t\\t\\t// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints\\n256: \\t\\t}\\n257: \\n258: \\t\\tif match {\\n259: \\t\\t\\t// increase the matchCount\\n260: \\t\\t\\tconstraintMatches++\\n261: \\n262: \\t\\t\\tswitch segmentMatchType {\\n263: \\t\\t\\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\\n264: \\t\\t\\t\\t// can short circuit here since we had at least one match\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n266: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n269: \\t\\t\\t}\\n270: \\t\\t} else {\\n271: \\t\\t\\t// no match\\n272: \\t\\t\\tswitch segmentMatchType {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n276: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\\n278: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n279: \\t\\t\\t}\\n280: \\t\\t}\\n281: \\t}\\n282: \\n283: \\tvar matched = true\\n284: \\n285: \\tswitch segmentMatchType {\\n286: \\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\\n287: \\t\\tif len(constraints) \\treturn resp, nil\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\\n224: // on the match circuit here since we had at least one match\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n266: \\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n269: \\t\\t\\t}\\n270: \\t\\t} else {\\n271: \\t\\t\\t// no match\\n272: \\t\\t\\tswitch segmentMatchType {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t// we can short circuit \\t\\treason = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"unknown match type %d\\\", segmentMatchType)\\n299: \\t\\tmatched = false\\n300: \\t}\\n301: \\n302: \\treturn matched, reason, nil\\n303: }\\n304: \\n305: func flipt.EvaluationReason_ERROR_EVALUATION_REASON\n164: \t\t\treturn resp, err\n165: \t\t}\n166: \n167: \t\tvar (\n168: \t\t\tvalidDistributions []*storage.EvaluationDistribution\n169: \t\t\tbuckets            []int\n170: \t\t)\n171: \n172: \t\tfor _, d := range distributions {\n173: \t\t\t// don't include 0% rollouts\n174: \t\t\tif d.Rollout > 0 {\n175: \t\t\t\tvalidDistributions = else {\n181: \t\t\t\t\tbucket := buckets[len(buckets)-1] + int(d.Rollout*percentMultiplier)\n182: \t\t\t\t\tbuckets = append(buckets, bucket)\n183: \t\t\t\t}\n184: \t\t\t}\n185: \t\t}\n186: \n187: is outside of our existing buckets then it does not match any distribution\n204: \t\tif index == len(validDistributions) {\n205: \t\t\tresp.Match = false\n206: \t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"did not match any distributions\")\n207: \t\t\treturn resp, nil\n208: \t\t}\n209: \n210: \t\td := validDistributions[index]\n211: \t\te.logger.Debug(\"matched distribution\", zap.Reflect(\"evaluation_distribution\", \t\treturn resp, nil\n218: \t} // end rule loop\n219: \n220: \treturn resp, nil\n221: }\n222: \n223: // matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\n224: // on the match type.\n225: func (e *Evaluator) matchConstraints(evalCtx map[string]string, constraints []storage.EvaluationConstraint, segmentMatchType flipt.MatchType, entityId string) (bool, string, error) {\n226: \tconstraintMatches := 0\n227: \n228: \tvar reason string\n229: \n230: \tfor _, c := range constraints {\n231: \t\tv := evalCtx[c.Property]\n232: \n233: \t\tvar (\n234: \t\t\tmatch bool\n235: \t\t\terr   error\n236: \t\t)\n237: \n238: \t\tswitch c.Type {\n239: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n240: \t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, v)\n241: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_NUMBER_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n242: \t\t\tmatch, err = matchesNumber(c, v)\n243: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_BOOLEAN_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n244: \t\t\tmatch, err = matchesBool(c, v)\n245: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_DATETIME_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n246: \t\t\tmatch, err = matchesDateTime(c, v)\n247: \t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_ENTITY_ID_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n248: \t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, entityId)\n249: \t\tdefault:\n250: \t\t\treturn false, reason, errs.ErrInvalid(\"unknown constraint type\")\n251: \t\t}\n252: \n253: \t\tif err != nil {\n254: \t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"error matching constraint\", zap.String(\"property\", c.Property), zap.Error(err))\n255: \t\t\t// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints\n256: \t\t}\n257: \n258: \t\tif match {\n259: \t\t\t// increase the matchCount\n260: \t\t\tconstraintMatches++\n261: \n262: \t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n263: \t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n264: \t\t\t\t// can short circuit here since we had at least one match\n265: \t\t\t\tbreak\n266: \t\t\tdefault:\n267: \t\t\t\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\n268: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n269: \t\t\t}\n270: \t\t} else {\n271: \t\t\t// no match\n272: \t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n273: \t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n274: \t\t\t\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\n275: \t\t\t\tbreak\n276: \t\t\tdefault:\n277: \t\t\t\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\n278: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n279: \t\t\t}\n280: \t\t}\n281: \t}\n282: \n283: \tvar matched = true\n284: \n285: \tswitch segmentMatchType {\n286: \tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n287: \t\tif len(constraints) != constraintMatches {\n288: \t\t\treason = \"did not match ALL constraints\"\n289: \t\t\tmatched = false\n290: \t\t}\n291: \n292: \tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n293: \t\tif len(constraints) > 0 && constraintMatches == 0 {\n294: \t\t\treason = \"did not match ANY constraints\"\n295: \t\t\tmatched = false\n296: \t\t}\n297: \tdefault:\n298: \t\treason = fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown match type %d\", segmentMatchType)\n299: \t\tmatched = false\n300: \t}\n301: \n302: \treturn matched, reason, nil\n303: }\n304: \n305: func crc32Num(entityID string, salt string) uint {\n306: \treturn uint(crc32.ChecksumIEEE([]byte(salt+entityID))) % totalBucketNum\n307: }\n308: \n309: const (\n310: \t// totalBucketNum represents how many buckets we can use to determine the consistent hashing\n311: \t// distribution and rollout\n312: \ttotalBucketNum uint = 1000\n313: \n314: \t// percentMultiplier implies that the multiplier between percentage (100) and totalBucketNum\n315: \tpercentMultiplier float32 = float32(totalBucketNum) / 100\n316: )\n317: \n318: func matchesString(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) bool {\n319: \tswitch c.Operator {\n320: \tcase flipt.OpEmpty:\n321: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n322: \tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n323: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n324: \t}\n325: \n326: \tif v == \"\" {\n327: \t\treturn false\n328: \t}\n329: \n330: \tvalue := c.Value\n331: \n332: \tswitch c.Operator {\n333: \tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n334: \t\treturn value == v\n335: \tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n336: \t\treturn value != v\n337: \tcase flipt.OpPrefix:\n338: \t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n339: \tcase flipt.OpSuffix:\n340: \t\treturn strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n341: \tcase flipt.OpIsOneOf:\n342: \t\tvalues := []string{}\n343: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n344: \t\t\treturn false\n345: \t\t}\n346: \t\treturn slices.Contains(values, v)\n347: \tcase flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:\n348: \t\tvalues := []string{}\n349: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n350: \t\t\treturn false\n351: \t\t}\n352: \t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, v)\n353: \t}\n354: \n355: \treturn false\n356: }\n357: \n358: func matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n359: \tswitch c.Operator {\n360: \tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n361: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n362: \tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n363: \t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n364: \t}\n365: \n366: \t// can't errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing number from %q\", v)\n374: \t}\n375: \n376: \tif c.Operator == flipt.OpIsOneOf {\n377: \t\tvalues := []float64{}\n378: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n379: \t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value for constraint %q\", c.Value)\n380: \t\t}\n381: \t\treturn slices.Contains(values, n), nil\n382: \t} else if c.Operator == flipt.OpIsNotOneOf {\n383: \t\tvalues := []float64{}\n384: \t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n385: \t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\t\t\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar matched = true\n\n\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\tif len(constraints) != constraintMatches flipt.OpEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n\tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n\t}\n\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tvalue := c.Value\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == v\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != v\n\tcase flipt.OpPrefix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesBool(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesDateTime(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn matchConstraints is a utility function that will return if all or any constraints have matched for a segment depending\n// on the match type.\nfunc (e *Evaluator) matchConstraints(evalCtx map[string]string, constraints []storage.EvaluationConstraint, segmentMatchType flipt.MatchType, entityId string) (bool, string, error) {\n\tconstraintMatches := 0\n\n\tvar reason string\n\n\tfor _, c := range constraints {\n\t\tv := evalCtx[c.Property]\n\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tmatch bool\n\t\t\terr   error\n\t\t)\n\n\t\tswitch c.Type {\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_STRING_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_NUMBER_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesNumber(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_BOOLEAN_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesBool(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_DATETIME_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch, err = matchesDateTime(c, v)\n\t\tcase flipt.ComparisonType_ENTITY_ID_COMPARISON_TYPE:\n\t\t\tmatch = matchesString(c, entityId)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn false, reason, errs.ErrInvalid(\"unknown constraint type\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\te.logger.Debug(\"error matching constraint\", zap.String(\"property\", c.Property), zap.Error(err))\n\t\t\t// don't return here because we want to continue to evaluate the other constraints\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif match {\n\t\t\t// increase the matchCount\n\t\t\tconstraintMatches++\n\n\t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\t\t\t// can short circuit here since we had at least one match\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// keep looping as we need to match all constraints\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// no match\n\t\t\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\t\t\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\t\t\t// we can short circuit because we must match all constraints\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t// keep looping to see if we match the next constraint\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar matched = true\n\n\tswitch segmentMatchType {\n\tcase flipt.MatchType_ALL_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\tif len(constraints) != constraintMatches {\n\t\t\treason = \"did not match ALL constraints\"\n\t\t\tmatched = false\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase flipt.MatchType_ANY_MATCH_TYPE:\n\t\tif len(constraints) > 0 && constraintMatches == 0 {\n\t\t\treason = \"did not match ANY constraints\"\n\t\t\tmatched = false\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treason = fmt.Sprintf(\"unknown match type %d\", segmentMatchType)\n\t\tmatched = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn matched, reason, nil\n}\n\nfunc crc32Num(entityID string, salt string) uint {\n\treturn uint(crc32.ChecksumIEEE([]byte(salt+entityID))) % totalBucketNum\n}\n\nconst (\n\t// totalBucketNum represents how many buckets we can use to determine the consistent hashing\n\t// distribution and rollout\n\ttotalBucketNum uint = 1000\n\n\t// percentMultiplier implies that the multiplier between percentage (100) and totalBucketNum\n\tpercentMultiplier float32 = float32(totalBucketNum) / 100\n)\n\nfunc matchesString(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) bool {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0\n\tcase flipt.OpNotEmpty:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0\n\t}\n\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tvalue := c.Value\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == v\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != v\n\tcase flipt.OpPrefix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpSuffix:\n\t\treturn strings.HasSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(v), value)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn slices.Contains(values, v)\n\tcase flipt.OpIsNotOneOf:\n\t\tvalues := []string{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, v)\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc matchesNumber(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tn, err := strconv.ParseFloat(v, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing number from %q\", v)\n\t}\n\n\tif c.Operator == flipt.OpIsOneOf {\n\t\tvalues := []float64{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value for constraint %q\", c.Value)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn slices.Contains(values, n), nil\n\t} else if c.Operator == flipt.OpIsNotOneOf {\n\t\tvalues := []float64{}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(c.Value), &values); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"Invalid value for constraint %q\", c.Value)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn !slices.Contains(values, n), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: we should consider parsing this at creation time since it doesn't change and it doesnt make sense to allow invalid constraint values\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseFloat(c.Value, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing number from %q\", c.Value)\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value == n, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn value != n, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLT:\n\t\treturn n < value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLTE:\n\t\treturn n <= value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGT:\n\t\treturn n > value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGTE:\n\t\treturn n >= value, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesBool(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := strconv.ParseBool(v)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"parsing boolean from %q\", v)\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpTrue:\n\t\treturn value, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpFalse:\n\t\treturn !value, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc matchesDateTime(c storage.EvaluationConstraint, v string) (bool, error) {\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpNotPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) == 0, nil\n\tcase flipt.OpPresent:\n\t\treturn len(strings.TrimSpace(v)) != 0, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// can't parse an empty string\n\tif v == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\td, err := tryParseDateTime(v)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\tvalue, err := tryParseDateTime(c.Value)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch c.Operator {\n\tcase flipt.OpEQ:\n\t\treturn value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpNEQ:\n\t\treturn !value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLT:\n\t\treturn d.Before(value), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpLTE:\n\t\treturn d.Before(value) || value.Equal(d), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGT:\n\t\treturn d.After(value), nil\n\tcase flipt.OpGTE:\n\t\treturn d.After(value) || value.Equal(d), nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn false, nil\n}\n\nfunc tryParseDateTime(v string) (time.Time, error) {\n\tif d, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v); err == nil {\n\t\treturn d.UTC(), nil\n\t}\n\n\tif d, err := func(*ListRequest[T])\n\n// ListWithQueryParamOptions takes a set of functional options for QueryParam and returns a ListOption\n// which applies them in order on the provided ListRequest.\nfunc ListWithQueryParamOptions[T any](opts ...QueryOption) ListOption[T] {\n\treturn func(r *ListRequest[T]) {\n\t\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n\t\t\topt(&r.QueryParams)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// PageParameter which exposes an additional offset\n// accessor for legacy paging implementations (page token supersedes).\ntype OffsetPageParameter interface {\n\tPageParameter\n\tGetOffset() int32\n}\n\n// ListWithParameters constructs a new ListRequest using the page parameters\n// exposed by the provided PageParameter implementation.\nfunc ListWithParameters[T any](t T, p PageParameter) *ListRequest[T] {\n\topts := []QueryOption{\n\t\tWithLimit(uint64(p.GetLimit())),\n\t\tWithPageToken(p.GetPageToken()),\n\t}\n\n\tif po, ok := p.(OffsetPageParameter); ok {\n\t\topts = append(opts, WithOffset(uint64(po.GetOffset())))\n\t}\n\n\treturn ListWithOptions[T](t, ListWithQueryParamOptions[T](opts...))\n}\n\n// ListWithOptions constructs a new ListRequest using the provided req\n}\n\n// Reference is a string which can refer to either a concrete\n// revision or it can be an indirect named reference.\ntype Reference string\n\n// ReferenceRequest is used to identify a flags[len(flags)-1]\n\t\tflags = flags[:req.QueryParams.Limit]\n\t}\n\n\tresults.Results = flags\n\n\tif next != nil {\n\t\tout, err := json.Marshal(PageToken{Key: next.Key, Offset: offset + uint64(len(flags))})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn results, fmt.Errorf(\"encoding page token %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tresults.NextPageToken = base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(out)\n\t}\n\n\treturn results, nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *Store) setVariants(ctx context.Context, namespaceKey string, flagsByKey map[string]*flipt.Flag) error {\n\tallFlagKeys := make([]string, 0, len(flagsByKey))\n\tfor k := updated_at\").\n\t\tFrom(\"variants\").\n\t\tWhere(sq.Eq{\"namespace_key\": namespaceKey, \"flag_key\": allFlagKeys}).\n\t\tOrderBy(\"created_at\")\n\n\trows, err := query.QueryContext(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif cerr := rows.Close(); cerr != nil && err == nil {\n\t\t\terr = cerr\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tfor rows.Next() {\n\t\tvar (\n\t\t\tvariant updated_at\").\n\t\t\tFrom(\"segments\").\n\t\t\tWhere(sq.Eq{\"namespace_key\": req.Predicate.Namespace()}).\n\t\t\tOrderBy(fmt.Sprintf(\"created_at %s\", req.QueryParams.Order))\n\t)\n\n\tif req.QueryParams.Limit > 0 {\n\t\tquery = query.Limit(req.QueryParams.Limit + 1)\n\t}\n\n\tvar offset uint64\n\n\tif req.QueryParams.PageToken != \"\" {\n\t\ttoken, err := decodePageToken(s.logger, req.QueryParams.PageToken)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn results, err\n\t\t}\n\n\t\toffset = token.Offset\n\t\tquery = query.Offset(offset)\n\t} else if req.QueryParams.Offset > 0 {\n\t\toffset = rows.Close(); cerr != nil && err == nil {\n\t\t\terr = cerr\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\t// keep track of segments we've seen so we don't append duplicates because of the join\n\tsegmentsByKey := segment)\n\t\tsegmentsByKey[segment.Key] = segment\n\t}\n\n\tif err := rows.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn results, err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := rows.Close(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn results, err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.setConstraints(ctx, req.Predicate.Namespace(), segmentsByKey); err != nil {\n\t\treturn results, err\n\t}\n\n\tvar next *flipt.Segment\n\n\tif len(segments) > nil {\n\t\tout, err := json.Marshal(PageToken{Key: next.Key, Offset: offset + uint64(len(segments))})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn results, fmt.Errorf(\"encoding page token %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tresults.NextPageToken = base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(out)\n\t}\n\n\treturn results, nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *Store) setConstraints(ctx context.Context, namespaceKey string, segmentsByKey map[string]*flipt.Segment) error {\n\tallSegmentKeys := make([]string, 0, len(segmentsByKey))\n\tfor k := range segmentsByKey {\n\t\tallSegmentKeys = append(allSegmentKeys, k)\n\t}\n\n\tquery := s.builder.Select(\"id, namespace_key, segment_key, type, property, operator, value, description, created_at, updated_at\").\n\t\tFrom(\"constraints\").\n\t\tWhere(sq.Eq{\"namespace_key\": namespaceKey, \"segment_key\": allSegmentKeys}).\n\t\tOrderBy(\"created_at\")\n\n\trows, err :=
